mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in the majority of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment falls below the estimated natural rate, markets price accelerated Fed tightening to preempt wage-driven inflation, raising terminal discount rates and compressing S&P 500 valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and retail sales generate a reinforcing recession signal that is stronger than either variable alone, causing risk-averse portfolio rebalancing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both register month-over-month declines
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict negative 20-day returns with an information coefficient below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During the zero lower bound period, an upside surprise in consumer price inflation forces markets to pull forward the expected date of Fed liftoff, increasing the short-term real interest rate trajectory and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.3 index points in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above the 1.2 million unit threshold signal durable residential investment momentum that propagates into construction employment and durable goods consumption, lifting S&P 500 earnings expectations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when HOUST is above 1200.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth coinciding with a federal funds rate below 0.25% indicates a Goldilocks macro environment where economic expansion is not yet constrained by monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary with above-trend employment growth
failure_condition: The combination of PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate revenue growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions follow output.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in the majority of quarters when INDPRO is rising month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above 0.15% signals the end of the zero lower bound and the start of monetary normalization, triggering a rapid repricing of near-term liquidity conditions and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a FEDFUNDS increase above 0.15%.
